question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (frequency field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the Duration.ofMillis(100L) frequency to cause a time-of-check-to-time-of-use (TOCTOU) race in EntityStakeCalculatorImpl.calculate(), under the precondition that two block processing cycles overlap at a staking period boundary, causing entityStakeRepository.updateEntityStake() to be called with stale entity_state_start data, resulting in incorrect pending_reward for accounts that received staking rewards in the boundary block?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (sourceType field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the AUTO mode's block node priority ordering (lower priority value = higher priority) to ensure a malicious auto-discovered node with priority=0 is always selected first, under the precondition that the attacker registers a block node with priority 0 and the legitimate nodes have higher priority values, causing all staking reward block data to be sourced from the attacker's node?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: getBucketName()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the network-to-bucket-name mapping in ImporterProperties.HederaNetwork.getBlockStreamBucketName() by operating on a non-mainnet/non-testnet network where the bucket name is not hardcoded, under the precondition that the deployment uses a custom network name, causing getBucketName() to return a predictable bucket name that the attacker has pre-registered, serving blocks with fraudulent staking reward transfers?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (stream field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the StreamProperties.readmitDelay = 1 minute to cause a 1-minute window where a legitimate block node is inactive and the FILE source is used, under the precondition that the attacker can cause a block node to fail and has pre-staged malicious block files in the cloud bucket, causing staking reward blocks processed during the readmit delay window to contain fraudulent data?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (enabled field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the transition from enabled=false to enabled=true in BlockProperties to cause a gap in block processing, under the precondition that the attacker can time the enabling of block stream to coincide with a staking period end, causing the NodeStakeUpdateTransaction block to be missed and pending_reward to not be updated for that period?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (autoDiscoveryEnabled field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the merge logic in BlockNodeDiscoveryService where auto-discovered nodes override config nodes with the same getMergeKey() (host+port+requiresTls), under the precondition that the attacker registers a block node with the same endpoint as a legitimate configured node but different TLS setting, causing the attacker's node properties to override the legitimate node configuration and serve manipulated staking reward blocks?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (stream field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the maxStreamResponseSize limit in StreamProperties to cause the importer to reject oversized streaming responses from legitimate block nodes, under the precondition that the attacker can inject large padding into block node responses via a man-in-the-middle position, causing the importer to fall back to FILE source and process attacker-staged block files with manipulated staking reward amounts?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/block/BlockProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Theft of unpaid staking rewards'] [Function: BlockProperties (sourceType field)] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that when sourceType=AUTO and both sources are unhealthy (errors >= 3), the system alternates between them on each call, under the precondition that the attacker can keep both sources in an unhealthy state, causing non-deterministic block processing where some staking reward blocks are processed from a malicious FILE source and others are skipped entirely?
